children.name: Buceros
children.description: Large, spectacular hornbills with enormous casques atop the bill, which in some species are solid and used for aerial combat. The genus includes the most iconic Asian hornbills, revered in many cultures as symbols of majesty and good fortune. Their wingbeats produce a distinctive whooshing sound audible from a distance.

children.name: Buceros bicornis
children.description: One of the largest and most recognisable hornbills, found in forests of the Indian subcontinent and Southeast Asia. It has a massive yellow-and-black casque, a black band across the white neck, and a striking white and black body. In many tribal cultures the casque is used in ornaments and the bird is the subject of numerous myths.

children.name: Buceros rhinoceros
children.description: Named for the prominent upturned casque that resembles a rhinoceros horn, this is one of the largest hornbills of Southeast Asian rainforests. It has a striking black body, white thighs and vent, and a bright yellow-orange bill and casque with red highlights. It is the state bird of the Malaysian state of Sarawak.

children.name: Buceros hydrocorax
children.description: A rufous hornbill endemic to the Philippines.

children.name: Buceros abyssinicus
children.description: A northern ground hornbill of the Sahel region.

children.name: Buceros leadbeateri
children.description: A large ground hornbill found in southern and East Africa.

children.name: Rhinoplax
children.description: A monotypic genus of endangered hornbill with a solid, front-heavy casque made of keratin, unlike the hollow casques of most other hornbills. The Helmeted Hornbill is unique for the intense red colour of the casque, which is produced by the bird preening its uropygial gland and rubbing the secretion into the casque.

children.name: Rhinoplax vigil
children.description: A critically endangered hornbill of the lowland rainforests of the Malay Peninsula, Sumatra, and Borneo. It has a solid casque of ivory that has been carved into ornaments for centuries, driving massive poaching pressure. Its loud, laughing call carries through the forest and is distinctive among the hornbill chorus.

children.name: Tockus
children.description: Small to medium-sized hornbills of African savannas and woodlands, typically with a long, decurved bill and a relatively small casque. They are the most terrestrial of the arboreal hornbills, often foraging on the ground for insects and small vertebrates.

children.name: Tockus leucomelas
children.description: A familiar hornbill of southern African savannas and dry woodlands, easily recognised by its long yellow bill. It has a white belly, spotted black-and-white wings, and a diagnostic dark eyestripe. It is featured in the popular documentary series Meerkat Manor, where it was immortally named a beloved character.

children.name: Tockus damarensis
children.description: A hornbill endemic to Namibia.

children.name: Tockus rufirostris
children.description: A red-billed hornbill found in southern Africa.

children.name: Tockus kempi
children.description: A hornbill found in West Africa.

children.name: Tockus flavirostris
children.description: A yellow-billed hornbill found in East Africa.

children.name: Tockus erythrorhynchus
children.description: A widespread hornbill with red bill, found in savannas of East Africa.

children.name: Tockus monteiri
children.description: A hornbill found in Angola and Namibia.

children.name: Tockus bradfieldi
children.description: A hornbill found in Angola and Namibia.

children.name: Tockus albirostris
children.description: A hornbill from West Africa.

children.name: Lophoceros
children.description: African hornbills characterised by a relatively small casque and a distinctive pale bill often with a dark tip or stripe. They are adaptable birds of savanna, woodland, and forest edge, and are among the most frequently encountered hornbills across Africa.

children.name: Lophoceros nasutus
children.description: Widespread across sub-Saharan Africa in savanna and open woodland. It has a uniform grey-brown body, a pale bill with a dark stripe on the upper mandible, and a white vent. It often perches conspicuously on dead branches, uttering a thin, piping whistled call.

children.name: Lophoceros pallidirostris
children.description: A hornbill with pale bill, found in southern Africa.

children.name: Lophoceros alboterminatus
children.description: A crowned hornbill of eastern and southern African forests.

children.name: Lophoceros fasciatus
children.description: A pied hornbill found in West and Central Africa.

children.name: Lophoceros hemprichii
children.description: A hornbill of the Horn of Africa.

children.name: Lophoceros jacksoni
children.description: A hornbill found in East Africa.

children.name: Lophoceros camerunensis
children.description: A hornbill from Cameroon.

children.name: Lophoceros griseus
children.description: A grey hornbill from West Africa.

children.name: Lophoceros hartlaubii
children.description: A hornbill from West and Central Africa.

children.name: Anthracoceros
children.description: Asian hornbills with glossy black plumage and large, heavy bills typically in contrasting shades of ivory, yellow and black. They are forest-dwellers that feed mainly on fruit but also take small animals. Their loud, raucous calls are a prominent sound in tropical Asian forests.

children.name: Anthracoceros albirostris
children.description: A small, common hornbill found from the Indian subcontinent through Southeast Asia to Borneo and the Philippines. It has glossy black plumage with a white belly and face, and a large yellow-white bill with a black patch on the side. It adapts well to secondary forests and urban areas, even nesting in cavities in buildings.

children.name: Anthracoceros malabaricus
children.description: A hornbill found in the Western Ghats of India.

children.name: Anthracoceros convexus
children.description: A hornbill found in the Malay Peninsula, Sumatra, and Borneo.

children.name: Anthracoceros marchei
children.description: A hornbill endemic to Palawan in the Philippines.

children.name: Anthracoceros montani
children.description: A critically endangered hornbill endemic to the Sulu Archipelago.

children.name: Rhyticeros
children.description: Large, predominantly frugivorous hornbills of Southeast Asian forests, characterised by a wrinkled or folded casque on the upper bill. Males have bright yellow or orange facial and throat skin, while females have blue or green skin. They are important seed dispersers for many rainforest trees.

children.name: Rhyticeros undulatus
children.description: A large hornbill of the Asian mainland and Greater Sundas, ranging from northeast India through Southeast Asia. The male has a creamy-white head and neck, a bright yellow gular pouch, and a low wrinkled casque atop the bill. It gathers in large roosting flocks that can number over a hundred birds.

children.name: Rhyticeros cassidix
children.description: The knobbed hornbill is endemic to the island of Sulawesi in Indonesia, where it inhabits lowland and montane forests. Males have a distinctive high, rounded casque and bright yellow facial skin. Its loud, barking call is a characteristic sound of Sulawesi forests.

children.name: Rhyticeros narcondami
children.description: The Narcondam hornbill is endemic to the small island of Narcondam in the Andaman Sea. It has a dark brown body, a white tail, and a pale yellow bill with a low casque. With a population of only a few hundred individuals, it is one of the most restricted-range hornbill species.

children.name: Rhyticeros plicatus
children.description: The Papuan hornbill is a large hornbill found across New Guinea and the surrounding islands. It has a creamy-white head and neck, a bright blue throat patch, and a low wrinkled casque. It is an important seed disperser in lowland rainforests.

children.name: Rhyticeros subruficollis
children.description: The plain-pouched hornbill is a medium-sized hornbill with a pale head, a dark body, and a distinctive plain throat pouch lacking the ridges of related species. It is found in lowland forests of the Malay Peninsula, Sumatra, and Borneo. It is classified as vulnerable due to habitat loss.

children.name: Rhyticeros everetti
children.description: A hornbill endemic to Sumba Island, Indonesia.

children.name: Ceratogymna
children.description: Large, predominantly black hornbills of West and Central African forests with striking blue facial skin and large casques. They are primarily frugivorous and play a crucial role in forest regeneration through seed dispersal.

children.name: Ceratogymna atrata
children.description: The black-casqued hornbill is a large, glossy black bird with a black bill and casque, bright blue facial skin, and a white-tipped tail. It is found in the lowland rainforests of West and Central Africa. It feeds primarily on fruit and is an important seed disperser.

children.name: Ceratogymna elata
children.description: The yellow-casqued hornbill is a large black bird with a striking yellow casque and bill, blue facial skin, and a white tail. It is found in the lowland forests of West Africa. Its loud, raucous call carries through the forest canopy.

children.name: Ceratogymna cylindricus
children.description: A hornbill from West Africa.

children.name: Bycanistes
children.description: Medium to large African hornbills with distinctive black-and-white plumage and large, often colourful casques. They are highly vocal and social, often moving through the forest canopy in small flocks. Their loud, piping calls are a characteristic sound of African forests.

children.name: Bycanistes bucinator
children.description: The trumpeter hornbill is a large black-and-white bird with a distinctive red or pinkish casque and a loud, trumpeting call. It is found in the forests and woodlands of eastern and southern Africa. It feeds primarily on fruit and is often seen in small, noisy flocks.

children.name: Bycanistes fistulator
children.description: The piping hornbill is a medium-sized black-and-white hornbill with a relatively small casque, found in the lowland forests of West and Central Africa. It is named for its loud, piping call that carries through the forest. It feeds on fruit and forages in the canopy.

children.name: Bycanistes cylindricus
children.description: The brown-cheeked hornbill is a large black-and-white bird with a brown cheek patch and a large ivory-coloured casque. It is found in the rainforests of West Africa from Sierra Leone to Ghana. It is considered near threatened due to habitat loss.

children.name: Bycanistes albotibialis
children.description: The white-thighed hornbill has distinctive white legs and lower belly, black upperparts, and a large pale casque. It is found in the lowland forests of Central Africa. It feeds on fruit in the canopy and is an important seed disperser.

children.name: Bycanistes brevis
children.description: The silvery-cheeked hornbill is a large black-and-white bird with a distinctive silvery-grey cheek patch and a large ivory-coloured casque. It is found in the montane forests of eastern Africa. Its loud, wailing call is a characteristic sound of East African highland forests.

children.name: Bycanistes subcylindricus
children.description: The black-and-white-casqued hornbill has a distinctive two-toned casque, black at the base and ivory at the tip, with black upperparts and white underparts. It is found in the forests of Central and East Africa. Its loud, far-carrying call consists of a series of hooting notes.

children.name: Bycanistes sharpii
children.description: A hornbill from West Africa.

children.name: Bycanistes temminckii
children.description: A hornbill from West Africa.

children.name: Berenicornis
children.description: A monotypic genus of striking white-crested hornbill found in Southeast Asian lowland forests. The single species is one of the most exquisitely plumaged hornbills, with a brilliant white head and tail contrasting with a black body.

children.name: Berenicornis comatus
children.description: The white-crowned hornbill has a stunning white head and crest, a black body, and a long white tail. It is found in lowland rainforests of the Malay Peninsula, Sumatra, and Borneo. Its call is a series of loud, barking notes, and it is considered vulnerable due to habitat loss.

children.name: Aceros
children.description: A genus of large, long-tailed hornbills found in the forests of South and Southeast Asia. Males have distinctive rufous or orange plumage on the head and neck, while females are black. They are important seed dispersers for many rainforest tree species.

children.name: Aceros nipalensis
children.description: The rufous-necked hornbill is a large hornbill with a rufous head and neck, a black body, and a pale bill with a series of ridges on the casque. It is found from the eastern Himalayas through Southeast Asia. It is classified as vulnerable and is one of the most threatened hornbills in Asia.
